title: Copper Falcon Dynamics
preview:
Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood. Initial onboarding via hosted API to validate latency/quality, scoped move to Dedicated (VPC) for production. Security review underway (SOC2 + SSO + audit logs + KMS).

Discovery highlights:
- Customer profile: B2B SaaS (product analytics SDK + workspace), NA HQ, EU users require residency.
- Primary workloads: chat (customer-facing assistant in-app), embeddings for search across event data, reranking for top-N results.
- Traffic profile: steady baseline ~150 qps embeddings/search, bursty chat peaks ~400 qps during release windows. Cost-sensitive for bursts.
- Latency targets: chat p95 < 200ms for 512-token flows, embedding gen < 80ms single-vector on avg.
- Model preference: prefer Llama-3-class 70B quantized int8 on Dedicated; acceptable fallback to 13B for lower-cost routes. Want routing policy: prefer low-latency (dedicated region) then fallback to hosted cheaper variant.

POC / eval plan (agreed):
- Phase 1 (hosted, 2 weeks): validate prompt templates, obs via Redwood Console, token-level cost breakdown. (Completed: Feb 2026)
- Phase 2 (dedicated pilot, 4 weeks): reserved 2x A100-equivalent, VPC peering test, SAML SSO integration with Okta, audit log streaming to their SIEM. (Planned start: 2026-03-15)
- Success criteria: chat p95 <200ms, embedding latency <80ms, no data exfil in VPC tests, cost per 1M tokens within agreed band.

Security / compliance notes (what Legal/Infra asked for):
- Must provide SOC2 Type II report + subprocessor list (legal won't sign until received).
- SSO: Okta IdP metadata, SAML integration docs, SCIM or Just-in-Time provisioning desirable.
- Audit logs: retention configurable, prefer 12+ months; need streaming to customer SIEM (Splunk).
- KMS: customer-managed key support required for Dedicated (bring-your-own-key) or HSM integration.
- Data residency: EU-in-region hosting for EU customers; confirm region routing and fallbacks.
- Retention controls: ability to set per-route retention and automatic purge for PII-ish traces.

Commercial / pricing convo:
- Asking for committed capacity discount tiers (monthly commit) vs on-demand hosted token rates.
- Wants model to include burst capacity clauses and an overage policy.
- Redwood asked for ARR estimate to size proposal: CF expects $125k-$250k ARR if SLA and residency checks out.

Recent timeline (CRM-style fragments):
- 2026-02-21: Intro call w/ Diego (AE) + Priya (SE). demo hosted API + streaming. fireflies ff_20260221_3a12.
- 2026-02-28: Hosted POC kicked off; collected sample prompts + evaluation set. ff_20260228_9b3f2a (recorded).
- 2026-03-03: Infra call: network asked for VPC peering diagram, ingress/egress ACLs, and peering SLA. Jira INF-2147 created.
- 2026-03-08: Legal sent SOC2 evidence request spreadsheet. Drive: SOC2-evidence-request-CopperFalcon.xlsx.

Quotes / verbatims from stakeholders:
- "We need provable audit trails for 12 months — product security is non-negotiable." — Head of Security
- "If latency is a hair over target on peak, we need automated fallback to cheaper instance to avoid failed requests." — CTO

Next steps (short):
- Send SOC2 Type II + subprocessor list to Legal (AE to follow up).
- SE to produce VPC peering diagram + egress controls doc by 2026-03-12.
- Schedule SOC2 evidence review call (target 2026-03-16) and dedicated pilot kickoff (2026-03-15 tentative).

Open questions for Redwood product team:
- Confirm BYOK/KMS workflow for Dedicated and key rotation schedule.
- Clarify audit log export formats and retention SLA options (CSV/JSON/Syslog/Splunk HEC).
- Provide example pricing for 2x A100-equivalent reserved cluster with 30% commit discount and burst add-on pricing.

Short CRM notes (shorthand):
- AE: Diego - high energy, pushing demo + SOC2 now.
- SE: Priya - will run POC config, needs sample prompts + token budget.
- Legal: waiting, gate for procurement.
- Infra: wants peering diagram + test window.

Attachments referenced: drive:/decks/CopperFalcon-Redwood-POC-pricing-v2.pdf (pricing sheet w/ two commit tiers), drive:/security/SOC2-evidence-request-CopperFalcon.xlsx (legal checklist).

Overall sentiment: serious buyer, mid-market cadence — will convert if Dedicated VPC + SOC2 deliverables are clean and pricing for bursty workloads is reasonable.

title: Meadowridge VertexSearch
preview:
CRM notes:
- Company snapshot: mid-market B2B SaaS (product search & recommendations).
- Primary contact: Sarah Lin (Head of Product), Eng lead: Martin Cho.
- Use-case details: embeddings-driven semantic search across product catalog + user behavioral signals; nightly ETL creates/upserts ~150k embeddings, real-time ingestion for new items (100-500/day).
- Requirements:
  * Throughput: 5k QPS target for peak search traffic; p95 latency target <100ms for search queries.
  * Batch jobs: nightly window must complete within 2 hours; currently using Airflow + Postgres (pgvector) -> considering switching heavy-indexing to vector DB (Pinecone) for scale.
  * Integration: need connectors for Pinecone and an option to keep warm caches in Redis; also must support pgvector fallback.
  * Model preferences: prefer open/Redwood-verified embeddings models (256-1024 dims) to avoid vendor lock; evaluating embedding dimension tradeoffs (cost vs quality).
  * Cost sensitivity: finance called out target unit cost ~<$0.0004/embedding at scale; committed capacity looked favorable if predictable.
- Security / compliance: requiring SSO (SAML), SOC2 evidence, audit logs shipped to SIEM, KMS for key management, and EU data residency for EU users.
- POC goals: confirm p95 latency for search under realistic mix, verify nightly ingestion throughput without throttling, measure cost per 1M embeddings both hosted and Dedicated.
- On the call Sarah said: "If we can get predictable latency and cut embedding cost by 30% it's a no-brainer."
- Internal notes: AE & SE recommend two-phase approach: (1) finish hosted POC for integration and quality checks; (2) run a 7-day Dedicated trial (reserved pool) to validate sustained throughput and economics.
- Decision criteria: latency SLO, cost per embedding, support for VPC peering and KMS, and SLA for uptime + incident response.
- Misc links: pricing deck shared (drive link), security FAQ sent, awaiting completed SOC2 questionnaire (due 2026-03-18).

title: LintCraft Codeworks
preview:
Summary: LintCraft builds a developer-facing code search + insights product used internally at customer orgs and as a hosted product. They operate ~2,200 active repos across customers (mix of mono-repos and microservices). Primary ask: scalable embeddings + reranking that keeps 95th-percentile latency < 300ms for top-k code search, cost < $0.02/query, supports daily re-indexing for active repos and weekly full re-index.

Technical constraints / requirements:
- Corpus size: ~22M files, ~150M stored embeddings currently (mixed dense + sparse metadata). Expect growth 3-5x in 12 months.
- Query profile: peak 120 qps sustained for short bursts, typical 30-50 qps. Many short queries (10-60 tokens).
- Latency SLO: 95p < 300ms (strict), p50 target 50-80ms for vector lookup + reranker.
- Throughput: want 1,000s of QPS per cluster in future; POC focused at 120 qps.
- Cost sensitivity: target avg cost per query <$0.02 (incl. embedding lookup + small cross-encoder rerank).
- Re-index cadence: incremental hourly for hot repos, daily for active set (~40k files/day), weekly full re-index. Need incremental embeddings + fast upserts.
- Model profile: prefer open models for embeddings (code-embedding variants) + small cross-encoder (200M-600M) for reranking. Will consider quantized variants.
- Indexing architecture wanted: cold/hot separation, sharded ANN (FAISS/HNSW) with warm replica for low-latency reads.
- Persistence: require VPC/private control plane, KMS integration for keys, audit logs forwarded to their SIEM, SAML SSO + SCIM provisioning.
- Retention: retention/erase controls, 90-day rolling retention for analytics, plus ability to purge repo-level data on demand.

POC scope (agreed):
- 2-week dedicated-node perf POC. Dataset: 10 representative repos (~12M files, 10M embeddings).
- Tests: cold-start reindex time, incremental upsert latency, QPS ramp to 120 sustained, 95p latency profiling, cost estimate per query based on token counts and GPU utilization.
- Deliverables: runbook, performance report (p50/p95/p99), recommended batching/quantization profile, fallback config for degraded capacity.

Recent call notes (fragments):
- Marta (CTO): "If 95p > 300ms we cannot use this product for live code search; latency kills dev flow."
- Eng lead (Ravi): wants to see incremental reindex throughput numbers and realistic cost math for reranker.
- Security (Anya): flagged SAML metadata + SCIM tests as blocker — needs completed questionnaire and test IdP endpoint.

Open questions / blockers:
- Infosec signoff timeline unclear (they require SOC2 + pen test report).
- GPU budget: procurement needs estimate for 3-month trial nodes before committing to Dedicated capacity.
- Reranker compute: prefer to test a quantized cross-encoder vs a two-stage bi-encoder+light rerank. Which has lower cost/latency tradeoff?

Next steps (short):
- Deliver POC runbook (Jordan/Priya) by 2026-03-12 — include data extract steps and infra reqs.
- Provide security artifacts: SOC2 summary + pen-test stub, share IdP test account for SAML (requested 2026-03-10).
- Prepare cost model: per-query cost projection for 120 qps baseline and 1x/3x growth scenarios (due 2026-03-14).

Stubs & links: pricing deck -> drive:/Pricing/LC_Estimate_2026.pptx, security questionnaire -> drive:/Security/LC_Security_Questionnaire_2026.pdf, POC runbook draft -> drive:/Lit/LC_POC_Runbook_v1.xlsx.

CRM style short notes (snippets):
- "POC needs hot-upsert test — Ravi insisted on <500ms upsert to enable daily reindexing"
- "CTO: won't accept hidden egress costs; want transparent unit economics"
- "SE to propose batching configs + suggested quantization profile for code embeddings"

title: Marigold LessonLoop
preview:
Inbound self-signup from classroom teacher founder. Tried free credits; small proof-of-concept using Hosted API.

Summary / context:
- Product: in-product classroom copilot for K-12 tutoring + teacher assist (lesson summarization, answer grading suggestions, paraphrase for parents).
- Primary technical need: cheap, reliable text generation + realtime moderation of student inputs.
- Current stack: Node/Next.js frontend, Firebase auth; exploring SAML for school district SSO later.
- Latency/throughput: low concurrency (~5-20 reqs/min); interactive responses should feel snappy — target ~300-600ms for short replies (single-turn).
- Cost sensitivity: founder says "we're scrappy — can't absorb high per-token bills." Wants guidance on batching, caching, and sampling smaller models.

Recent activity (timeline):
- 2026-03-01: Self-registered, used free credits to call text-generation endpoint; left inbound contact form.
- 2026-03-03: AE (Jordan) intro email + quick security questionnaire sent.
- 2026-03-04: 25-min exploratory call (Fireflies ff_2026-03-04_maz3bq). Demoed simple lesson-summarize prompt; showed cost on sample prompt.
- 2026-03-06: Follow-up email with token-usage breakdown and suggested prompt tweaks. Customer responded with usage patterns and a short sample dataset.
- 2026-03-08: SE sync scheduled for technical walkthrough (Priya). Requested example of retention/deletion API and audit log format.

Requirements / ask from Marigold:
- Hosted API only for now (no Dedicated/Private).
- Clear cost controls (per-route token limits, sample thresholds).
- Built-in moderation endpoint or rule-set to filter profanity and personal data before showing teacher view.
- Ability to delete student conversation data programmatically and a retention policy they can present to district IT.
- Lightweight SAML SSO readiness for pilot district (timeline 2-3 months).

Quote / customer language snippets:
- "We love how fast we could get started, but tokens add up — need a predictable plan."
- "Must show district IT we can purge kids' chat history on request."

POC plan proposed:
- 2-week sandbox using Hosted API with sample 500-student dataset (anonymized).
- Deliverables: integration doc, 10 prompt templates, moderation rules example, monthly cost projection at 10k messages/month.
- Success criteria: avg latency <600ms for short replies, moderation false-positive rate <5% on benchmark set, total monthly run cost within estimated range.

Next steps (short):
- Send tailored 1-page pricing with burst-control tips (Jordan) by 2026-03-10.
- Technical walkthrough with Priya week of 2026-03-09 to cover batching, caching, and retention API.
- Provide SOC2/attestation summary and audit-log sample (security doc link).

Open blockers / notes for internal folks:
- Will need to emphasize cost-control recipes (model selection + temperature + token trimming).
- SE should prepare a short demo of moderation + retention API (code snippets in Node).
- Consider offering a small credits top-up or pilot discount to move from discovery -> qualified.

title: MezzoQuery Labs
preview:
Account snapshot: mid-market e-commerce search SaaS. Core product = storefront product discovery; currently BM25 + light learning-to-rank. Exploring LLM-based reranking for top-10 results.

Recent activity:
- 2026-02-10 discovery call (Samir) — product team + CTO on call. They described current infra: search index on Elastic, candidate generation at 250 qps, currently rerank offline with light GBDT. Interested in replacing offline heavy feature pipeline with LLM reranker for better long-tail relevance. (fireflies ff_20260210_mezzoquery_intro)
- 2026-03-02 POC kickoff with Redwood hosted API. Agreed scope: implement rerank endpoint that consumes topK candidates and returns score + prefer: structured reason tags. Validate accuracy uplift on holdout eval set (MRR and NDCG). (fireflies ff_20260302_poc_kickoff)
- 2026-03-04 exchanged pricing worksheet; asked for Dedicated pricing scenarios for 3 capacity tiers + burst. (gmail thread-CAK3zmL7-20260304-pricing)

POC details / success criteria:
- Offline scoring pipeline: produce rerank scores for 1M eval pairs in 2-3 days using batch export (they'll run locally with Redwood offline scoring bundle).
- Online latency target for rerank endpoint: p50 < 60ms for topK=10, p95 < 180ms. Peak QPS target ~300 with autoscaling.
- Quality target: +6-8% relative MRR on weighted product queries, NDCG@10 uplift on merchandising queries.
- Cost sensitivity: want estimate of token cost per request and per-1M queries. Primary decision driver: cost per useful uplift.

Security / compliance:
- Must support SSO/SAML for console access, audit logs for all inference requests, KMS key integration for model artifacts. Data residency preference: US only for first phase. Security team scheduled architecture review the week of 2026-03-15.

Open questions (from call):
- Can Redwood Dedicated expose a private VPC endpoint for batch/offline scoring jobs?
- What controls exist for model variant rollouts and automatic fallback to smaller quantized model under capacity pressure?
- Confirmation on KV cache behavior for rerank-style short-context calls (do we get cache benefit for repeated candidate tokens?).

Next actions:
- Samir to share full eval set export (1M rows) via Drive — DONE (link above).
- Priya (SE) to deliver Dedicated pricing scenarios and run a short hosted API perf run for topK=10 until 2026-03-14.
- Schedule security architecture review with Redwood infra (security thread = thread-CAJ9xQ2X-20260212-RELEVANCE-PROC).

Sales notes / tone: technical buyer + product manager. Decision timeline: POC through May, procurement in June. They like the transparency on per-token economics and observability in Redwood Console.

Stubs / artifacts referenced:
- Pricing worksheet: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Mezzo/Pricing-worksheet
- Eval set snapshot: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1EvalSet-2026-03-Mezzo.zip
- Jira escalations for perf tests: INF-4821 (batch scoring timeout), INF-4976 (model artifact upload error)

CRM shorthand / snippets from calls:
- "If we can get a predictable cost per uplift, we can justify Dedicated commit." — CTO
- "Need to run 1M offline scoring pass to validate latency/cost tradeoffs." — Lead ML Eng
- "Prefer US-only residency for phase 1." — Security lead

- 2026-02-10 discovery call (Samir) — product team + CTO on call. They described current infra: search index on Elastic, candidate generation at 250 qps, currently rerank offline with light GBDT. Interested in replacing offline heavy feature pipeline with LLM reranker for better long-tail relevance. (fireflies ff_20260210_mezzoquery_intro)
- 2026-03-02 POC kickoff with Redwood hosted API. Agreed scope: implement rerank endpoint that consumes topK candidates and returns score + prefer: structured reason tags. Validate accuracy uplift on holdout eval set (MRR and NDCG). (fireflies ff_20260302_poc_kickoff)

- Offline scoring pipeline: produce rerank scores for 1M eval pairs in 2-3 days using batch export (they'll run locally with Redwood offline scoring bundle).
- Online latency target for rerank endpoint: p50 < 60ms for topK=10, p95 < 180ms. Peak QPS target ~300 with autoscaling.
- Quality target: +6-8% relative MRR on weighted product queries, NDCG@10 uplift on merchandising queries.
- Cost sensitivity: want estimate of token cost per request and per-1M queries. Primary decision driver: cost per useful uplift.

Security / compliance:
- Must support SSO/SAML for console access, audit logs for all inference requests, KMS key integration for model artifacts. Data residency preference: US only for first phase. Security team scheduled architecture review the week of 2026-03-15.

- Can Redwood Dedicated expose a private VPC endpoint for batch/offline scoring jobs?
- What controls exist for model variant rollouts and automatic fallback to smaller quantized model under capacity pressure?
- Confirmation on KV cache behavior for rerank-style short-context calls (do we get cache benefit for repeated candidate tokens?).

- Priya (SE) to deliver Dedicated pricing scenarios and run a short hosted API perf run for topK=10 until 2026-03-14.

preview:

Evaluating Redwood Private (on-prem VPC + air-gapped variant) for downstream document search, trial report summarization, and assisted manuscript drafting. Primary driver: eliminate data egress risk and meet EU/US residency for certain study datasets. Infosec: high bar — full SOC2+ISO27001 evidence requested. title: Halcyon Security Orchestrators
preview: Summary: Halcyon is evaluating a Private (VPC) deployment to host sensitive transaction-support assistants and embeddings for compliance search. Primary procurement driver is auditable, exportable audit logs + customer-managed keys. Key asks: 1) SIEM export paths (Splunk/Datadog/Elastic) via secure forwarder; support for syslog/HTTPS with mutual-TLS; ability to push JSON and CEF formats. 2) Canonical log schema — want fields: timestamp, request_id, tenant_id, principal_id (pseudonymized), model, model_version, route, prompt_hash, response_hash, token_count_in, token_count_out, latency_ms, status_code, host/pod_id, region, client_ip (CIDR or masked), audit_action. Must be able to include redaction markers for PII removals. 3) Retention policy — SOC requires 7 years for audit trail metadata for transactional workflows; access logs (proxy/access) 1 year; debug/full payload logs kept only for 90 days and require explicit approval. Need WORM/immutable storage option for audit trail. 4) KMS integration — external key support (BYOK), HSM-backed keys, envelope encryption, key rotation policy and rotation proof artifacts, support for key import/export constraints. 5) Access controls & telemetry — SSO (SAML), fine-grained RBAC, admin audit trails, and ability to export audit logs to customer SIEM daily and on-demand. POC asks: demonstrate log shipping to customer Splunk with provided index mapping; show KMS BYOK flow with a sample key, prove that model cache artifacts are encrypted (metadata only), and validate that enabling audit logging adds <10% median latency overhead in our perf tests. Notes from SOC: "must see sample log files mapped to our Splunk sourcetypes; prefer CEF for legacy; JSON ok if mapping provided." Legal concerns: key escrow and term-end key destruction wording. Pricing: leaning toward a 12-month private deployment commit if security gating items are satisfied. Next deliverables: SIEM mapping doc (this week), KMS integration runbook + sample encrypted artifact, POC plan with perf targets and success criteria.

title: Emberbrook SaaS
preview:
Founder-led lead: Ava Rodriguez, ex-early stage startup CTO. Very price-conscious; building a PLG support chat inside existing web app.

Use-case: chat-first support assistant + doc search embeddings for help center. Plan: start hosted API trial, then move to reserved if volume grows.

Quoted: "If you can't beat or match OpenAI price/latency I can't justify switching — but I like the observability and routing features."

Primary ask: clear apples-to-apples pricing comparison (per-token + embedding unit) vs OpenAI and Anthropic. Wants sample invoice for 250k tokens/mo and 1M embedding units.

Security: SOC2 required soon; currently SSO via providers (Okta). Need audit logs and retention controls. No air-gapped requirement.

Next: prepare 3 scenario price sheet (low/typical/high usage), sample tokenized cost calc, and schedule 30m follow-up for pricing walkthrough next week.

title: Havenwell Clinical Search
preview:
CRM notes (synthesized) - compressed bullets and call paraphrase.

Account background:
- Enterprise healthtech platform, supports ambulatory EHR integrations and a clinical documentation archive with PHI.
- ~120M indexed docs (notes, reports, imaging captions) today; expect 3x growth in 18 months.
- Strong data retention requirements (7+ years) and strict egress controls.

Primary ask / product fit:
- Replace brittle keyword search with RAG: dense embeddings + cross-encoder reranker for high precision at top-K.
- Throughput target: 120 QPS sustained for peak business hours; peak spikes to 400 QPS for surge.
- Latency SLO: p95 end-to-end < 450ms for clinical search queries; soft p99 < 900ms.
- Cost sensitivity: target <$0.015 per user query (inclusive of retrieval + rerank).
- Index scale concerns: embeddings store ~200M vectors, average vector dim 768; expect storage + retrieval cost optimization.

Security / deployment constraints:
- Must sign BAA; HIPAA compliance non-negotiable.
- VPC peering or on-prem appliance preferred; no public internet egress for PHI.
- Audit log retention 7 years, immutable logs, ID-level access trails required.
- KMS-backed encryption keys under customer control; HSM option desirable for private deployment.
- SSO via SAML; SCIM provisioning for ops users.

Technical preferences and eval criteria:
- Want Redwood Private (VPC) or Dedicated if Private install window too long.
- Candidate retrieval: approximate k-NN (ANN) with caching of clinical prefixes; reranker: cross-encoder fine-tuned on clinician relevance labels.
- Evaluation metrics: MRR and P@1 prioritized; clinical safety checks for hallucination rate.
- Accepts quantized embeddings if quality delta <2% MRR.

POC plan and timeline:
- POC scope: 10M-record subset (oncology + cardiology), 2-week indexing + evaluation.
- Deliverables: baseline naive BM25 vs dense recall, rerank model (cross-encoder) candidate improvement, latency/cost dashboard.
- Success criteria: +15% absolute P@1 over BM25, p95 latency <450ms on 100 QPS, cost <= target.
- Proposed timeline: architecture review wk of 2026-03-15; index seed data delivered by Havenwell on 2026-03-22; POC complete by 2026-04-05.

Recent activity / timeline (CRM timeline):
- 2026-02-18: Intro arch call (Fireflies ff-20260218-3458-havenwell-archcall). AE (Maya) + SE (Diego) + Head of Data (L. Park).
- 2026-02-25: Sent security FAQ + initial network diagram (drive:/Havenwell/Redwood/security-faq.docx).
- 2026-03-01: Legal asked for BAA template and encryption SLA details; internal legal kickoff.
- 2026-03-05: Demo of rerank prototype against small search set; clinical SMEs liked relevance improvements but asked about auditability.
- 2026-03-08: Redwood provided rough-cost model for Dedicated vs Private; Havenwell requested breakdown per-query including rerank cost.

Quotes / direct paraphrases:
- "Top-1 accuracy matters more than raw throughput — clinicians need the right note first." — L. Park, Head of Data.
- "We cannot have queries traverse public endpoints for PHI; need VPC or on-prem arrangement." — CTO.

Open technical questions (from SE):
- How does Redwood preserve per-request audit trails across dedicated GPUs and fallbacks? Need sample audit log format.
- Can we pin reranker to a dedicated node pool to guarantee p95 latency under load?
- Options for local KMS/HSM integration for Private deployment.

Next steps (short):
- Schedule architecture review (AE + SE + infra) wk of 2026-03-15.
- Provide test dataset checklist and SFTP credentials for seed indexing.
- Legal: push BAA template and encryption SLAs; hold kickoff with Havenwell legal.

Internal notes (sales play):
- High upside ARR; likely Dedicated or Private contract.
- Leverage Redwood Optimize to propose emb quantization + cache config to meet cost target.
- Offer canary plan: start Dedicated with VPC peering then migrate to Private if legal requires on-prem.

Stubs & artifacts referenced:
- Security FAQ (drive:/Havenwell/Redwood/security-faq.docx)
- Pricing deck v2 (drive:/Havenwell/Redwood/pricing-deck-v2.pdf)
- Support ticket INF-2147 opened for network requirements

Short-hand next-step line for AE: "Arch review + send POC checklist — Maya to confirm 3/15 slot."

title: Labwell QueryLane
preview:
Inbound from docs search demo on website. Small engineering org (Backend: 2, Fullstack: 3, ML: 1) building an internal knowledge assistant for corporate docs and contracts. Interested primarily in self-serve Hosted API to get started; wants quick POC before committing. Key asks: hosted embeddings + a simple reranking flow (embed -> vector DB -> candidate fetch -> rerank with cross-encoder). Considering Pinecone/Weaviate for vector store but asked if Redwood hosted embeddings and reranking can replace some of that stack.

Call notes (2026-03-01):
- Customer: "Need something that works in days not weeks."
- They have ~250k documents (mix: policies, contracts, support articles). Average doc length long; plan to chunk ~800-1,000 tokens.
- Embedding requirements: prefer 1536-dim if available; want ability to lower precision/quantize for cost.
- Reranking: they expect a top-50 candidate fetch then cross-encoder rerank to top-5. Concerned about compute cost of cross-encoder on Hosted (cost + latency).
- Latency/throughput targets: target median query latency <120ms for vector search + rerank under 10 qps; black box target 95th <350ms for single-user queries.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget, looking for ~$1k/month POC cap.
- Data residency: need NA-only storage for POC, possible EU later.
- Security: SSO/SAML for their admin console; audit logs required for compliance.

They asked about:
- Whether Redwood Hosted embeddings allow direct metadata filters and per-role access control.
- Bundled pricing vs per-token for reranker.
- Example ingest pipeline for large doc stores and recommended chunk sizes.

AE notes: good fit for Hosted API self-serve + Optimize recommendations later. Recommend demo env with: hosted embeddings (1536), Pinecone/Weaviate quickstart vs Redwood vector routing doc, and a small rerank test using a lower-cost model for re-ranker.

title: Driftwood AI Integration
preview:
Lead source: self-serve signup (marketing site CTA).

Initial context:
- Small ecommerce platform (B2C) building conversational product finder + post-purchase support bot and personalized recommendations.
- Signed up with free credits 2026-02-18; poked around embeddings and streaming chat endpoints.
- Wants fast examples — 'give me a drop-in LangChain + Vercel example we can paste into Next.js' (quote from CTO).

Technical requirements / asks from call (2026-02-24):
- Primary integration targets: LangChain (Agents + RetrievalQA), LlamaIndex for document indexing, and Vercel AI SDK for edge serverless deploys.
- POC scope: index ~12k product docs (S3), power site search + contextual product chat.
- Latency target: ~<500ms end-user response for chat (95th pct) and <120ms per-embedding for single-vector calls (cost-sensitive).
- Throughput: bursty, ~20 req/s during promotions; mostly low traffic otherwise.
- Model preferences: open quantized models acceptable if Redwood hosted variants available; cost sensitivity — prefer smaller Llama-family or gpt-j-type quantized variants for embeddings/generic chat.
- Routing/fallback: want automated fallback to cheaper model during spikes.

Security / compliance notes:
- Needs SSO/SAML for admin users and audit logging. SOC2 compliance is a plus (customer security folks asked).
- No strict data residency requirement today; public cloud deploy OK.
- Requested documentation: security FAQ + endpoint level retention controls. (We shared /security/redwood-smb-faq.pdf — link in Drive).

Timeline & activities:
- 2026-02-18: self-serve signup, created first API key.
- 2026-02-20: AE outreach (Jordan) — intro email, asked about use-case. Gmail thread: thread:1782a9b2c5e.
- 2026-02-24: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_20260224_3112) w/ CTO + lead eng — demoed LangChain quickstart and streaming.
- 2026-02-26: Sent Vercel adapter quickstart + example repo link (drive folder).
- 2026-03-02: Follow-up: engineering hit blocker with SSO and uncertain about rate limits for embeddings; opened support ticket SUP-4128.

POC plan (customer-proposed): 2-week sprint
- Week 1: integrate Redwood hosted embeddings into existing LangChain retrieval pipeline; verify recall on product docs.
- Week 2: wire up Vercel AI SDK in Next.js to call Redwood chat endpoint (streaming) + run live A/B on product finder.

Ask list (what they want from Redwood):
- Sample adapters: ready-to-drop-in code for LangChain v0.1x, LlamaIndex indexing pipeline pointing to S3, and Vercel AI SDK example using Redwood-hosted_API with streaming.
- A short 'how to' on batching & caching recommendations to reduce token spend (suggested thresholds, KV cache hints).
- Clear published rate limits / burst guidance for hosted_api for SMB tier.
- Simple canary rollback examples / suggestion for model fallback config.

Sales notes / positioning tips for AE/SE:
- Emphasize quickstarts and small-code adapters — this team prefers copy-paste examples. Provide repo they can fork.
- Highlight cost controls (batching, quantization advice) and an easy path to Dedicated if traffic grows.
- Offer 1:1 pairing session with SE (Priya) to walk through LangChain + Vercel setup; propose a 60–90m pair-programming slot.

Next steps (agreed):
- SE to deliver LangChain + LlamaIndex + Vercel sample repo (Priya) by 2026-03-05.
- Jordan to schedule pairing session for week of 2026-03-08.
- Customer to provide SSO metadata for sandbox if they decide to promote POC beyond dev keys.

Open blockers (customer):
- Low internal engineering bandwidth — asked for minimal-effort SDK usage (Vercel example must be extremely lightweight).
- Need clarification on hosted API rate limits and per-embedding latency expectations.
- Security team requested SOC2 evidence and short note on retention controls (we promised drive doc).

Quick wins shared in thread:
- 1-page quickstart: 'LangChain + Redwood hosted API (Next.js + Vercel)' (in Drive).
- Small code snippet for streaming chat + function-calling included in email thread.

title: Ceres Pronto Inference
preview:
Account background: Ceres Pronto is a payments marketplace (mid-market fintech) building a real-time offer engine and conversational payment assistant. Heavy sensitivity to tail latency; most revenue-critical flows run at <100ms p95 in production. They currently run a mix of CPU microservices and lightweight GPU endpoints in-house.

Primary asks: 
- p95 latency <= 60ms for short-chat + reranking path (prompt length ~80 tokens).
- Sustained throughput target: 100–250 QPS during peak, spiky bursts to 1k QPS expected.
- Cost constraint: need clear unit cost model; target gross monthly LLM spend <$25k for pilot.
- Strong interest in batching and KV prefix caching to reduce token cost without breaking latency SLOs.
- Will evaluate int8/4-bit quantized variants for Dedicated/Private to hit cost targets.

Timeline & recent activity: 
- 2026-02-18: Intro demo w/ Marco + Priya; demo notes in ff_2026-02-18_7890.
- 2026-02-25: Security questionnaire sent; awaiting answers on KMS/HSM from infra (drive link).
- 2026-03-01: Deep-dive on batching/caching strategies; agreed to run perf POC (see ff_2026-03-01_8123).
- 2026-03-04: Provided preliminary cost model; customer asked for per-route breakdown and canary plan.

Technical preferences & constraints: 
- Preferred models: open-Llama family and quantized LLMs — want Redwood to recommend quantization profiles per workload.
- Routing: need regional routing (NA primary, EU fallback) and automatic fallback to smaller model under capacity pressure.
- Observability: token-level cost breakdown and per-route latency heatmaps required for billing + SLOs.
- Security: must integrate with corporate SSO (SAML), KMS for key management, audit logs retained 1 year, and EU data residency for EU users.

Benchmarking requests: 
- They want an official perf benchmark run against their replayed production traffic (anonymized) and a Redwood-provided microbenchmark harness. Must include: p50/p95/p99, throughput curves, GPU utilization, KV cache hit rates, and cost per 1M tokens for each model/quantization config.
- Request: baseline on hosted (for comparison), then Dedicated with batching tuned, then quantized Private run.

Quotes & user color: 
- "We can't tolerate >60ms p95 for payments accept path — that loses conversions." — Head of Product.
- AE note: "Very pragmatic team; they'll accept slightly higher median latency if tail is nailed and costs are predictable."

Next steps (short): 
- Send POC SOW & benchmark plan (Marco).
- Provision test Dedicated pool with GPU type recommended by Redwood (Priya to coordinate).
- Get security team answers on KMS/HSM and residency (attach drive doc).

Open questions: 
- Exact prod traffic shape for synthetic replay (they will provide telemetry export).
- Acceptance criteria for POC: p95 <=60ms AND cost <= 30% of current baseline.

Attachments & refs: pricing deck (drive:/redwood/proposals/ceres_pronto_pricing_v2.pdf), security QA (drive:/redwood/security/ceres_pronto_security_questions.docx).

CRM shorthand / call log snippets: 
- "Perf harness required — send benchmark steps + sample JMX/trace outputs."
- "Will consider Private if data residency + KMS checks out."
- "Procurement: legal wants a 30-day SLA trial clause."

title: Pelican Stone Interactive
preview:
Inbound SMB trial — customer runs a mid-sized ecommerce brand. Self-serve signup, started with hosted API.

Problem summary:
- Primary concern: chat latency for EU shoppers (they're NA HQ but ~40% traffic from EU).
- Reported behavior: intermittent spikes where response round-trip hits 900ms-1.2s (per-response), causing UI drop/abandon on support popups. CTO: "users churn when reply >700ms".

What we saw / debugging so far:
- They tested from eu-west-1 endpoints and routed some requests through a CDN/proxy — adds variability.
- Default SDK timeout left at 30s; front-end gives up earlier (~5s) but perceived latency is on first byte.
- Prompts include long conversation history + 2k tokens of system/context on many requests. No prefix caching configured.
- Model in use: mid-sized open checkpoint (latency-profiled) — good throughput but not pinned to region.

SE recommendations given on call:
- Region choice: suggest routing EU traffic to eu-west-1 for residency, but for latency testing try us-east-1 from their EU edge to compare (we saw median RTT reduce when serving from nearest Redwood region in the trial account).
- Timeouts: set SDK request timeout to 15s for server-to-server, but front-end socket timeout to 2.5s for progressive streaming fallback. Encourage streaming so UX can display partial tokens faster.
- Prompt guidance: truncate long system/context to most recent 6 turns, move static context to embeddings/doc-search layer or store in KV cache. Aim to reduce average input tokens by ~40-60% where possible.
- Max_tokens and response length: cap outputs to 150-200 tokens for support snippets; for long-form summaries use background job with relaxed timeout.
- Caching: enable prefix/KV cache for frequent customer FAQs. This should reduce per-token overhead for repeated questions.
- Batching: not recommended for low-concurrency interactive chat, but enable small batching (2-4) for peak windows to amortize kernel launch cost.

Action items (owner):
- Priya N. (SE): send a short config snippet they can paste into their SDK with region override, timeout values, and streaming example. (done 2026-03-02, link on Drive)
- Jordan (AE): follow up with pricing note on burst capacity and small committed tier to cap latency at peak.
- Customer infra (CTO): provide tracer logs for one sample request (requested 2026-03-03).

Quotes / customer voice:
- CTO: "We want predictable 200-400ms 90th percentile for primary support path. Anything above 700ms = bad UX."
- Product PM: "If Redwood can give us a short checklist to tune without infra changes we can roll to 80% of sessions quickly."

POC / evaluation plan:
- Quick two-week tuning plan: day 0-2: apply SDK region + timeout; day 3-7: enable caching + prompt truncation; day 8-14: measure and iterate.
- Expected outcome: reduce median latency by ~30-50% for typical queries and lower variance.

Next steps (short):
- Send tuned prompt examples + region recommendation (Jordan) — ETA 2026-03-06.
- Book 30m follow-up demo to verify improvements (aim week of 2026-03-08).

Blockers right now:
- Waiting on their infra team to share tracer logs and SSO metadata for the security doc.
- Pricing question on burst capacity for SMB — need quick reference deck.

References:
- Perf tuning deck: drive:/decks/pelicanstone-perf-tuning-v1.pdf
- Fireflies transcript: ff_20260301_abc123
- Support ticket: INF-1292

title: Lodestar Product Analytics
preview:
Lead: Lodestar (product analytics for consumer SaaS). Mid-market, heavy personalization use-case. Initially trialing Hosted API for quick integration (chat + reranking + embeddings), now evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation. Key pain: token costs during nightly re-ranks + real-time personalization latency spikes. AE ran preliminary cost model during demo; engineering asked for a cost workshop.

POC summary (short): integrated hosted endpoints into feature service, basic prefix caching enabled, measured end-to-end p99 latency 180-220ms on gen-1 prompts, cost runway looks tight at current call volumes. Team quote: "We need costs to be predictable before we expand to 10M monthly users."

Security: require SSO/SAML, SOC2 attestation, KMS integration and audit logging. Also data residency constraints for EU customers (want VPC + regional routing).

Commercial: price sensitivity flagged by CFO — asking for uplift on unit economics if commit to annual Dedicated. Interested in burst options for Black Friday-like events. Wants clear breakdown: per-token hosted vs dedicated reserved GPU amortized + cache savings.

Customer tone: pragmatic, engineering-first, wants numbers. Short timelines: procurement by 2026-05 if POC and cost workshop outcomes acceptable.

Stated next steps (from call): 1) Redwood to provide Dedicated sizing + annual pricing sheet; 2) run cost optimization workshop (batching/caching/quantization) with Lodestar infra team; 3) SE to run small quantization POC on 7B+ model variants and report quality delta.

title: Hedgerow Insight Labs
preview:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS. Evaluating Hosted API for quick integration, then planning Dedicated VPC for production (data residency + network isolation).
- Primary contact: CTO (Marcus Reed); product lead: Amira Patel. AE owner Priya N.; SE Ethan supporting. CSM tentatively assigned post-contract (Lena C.).

POC / Demo history:
- 2026-01-20 discovery call (ff_2026-01-20_hedgerow_discussion). Customer wanted embeddings + semantic search first; low-latency generative for in-app help later.
- 2026-01-25 sandbox integration with Hosted API (2 engineers). Quick wins: embeddings indexing of docs; reranker improved click-through on support results.
- 2026-02-12 hosted API demo w/ streaming + function-calling (ff_2026-02-12_poc-demo). Customer impressed by latency but flagged cost at scale.
- 2026-02-18 internal spike: batch vs prefix cache reduced token costs ~18% on their logs workload. SE ran batch config recommendations.

Requirements / constraints (customer-stated):
- Workloads: embeddings for search (primary), chat-support (secondary), reranking for product recommendations.
- Throughput target: sustained ~70-100 reqs/sec for embedding + search traffic during peak; bursts to 300rps for chat during flash events.
- Latency SLO: p95 < 350ms for embeddings lookup; p95 < 200ms target for chat generator. Quoted on calls: \"can't tolerate >200ms p95 for help chat\" (Marcus).
- Cost sensitivity: mid-market budget; prefers predictable committed monthly commit (prefer 12-month). Target range discussed ~$10k-20k/mo.
- Model preference: open/quantizable models (7B-70B families) with option for higher-quality variant for chat fallback. Interested in Redwood suggestions for quantization and kernel profiles.

Security / compliance ask (primary reason moving to Dedicated/VPC):
- SOC2 Type II evidence and specific SOC2 scope for Dedicated nodes.
- SSO / SAML integration required (IdP: Okta). Need SP metadata example and test plan.
- Audit logs: must be able to forward inference logs (metrics + request ids) to customer Splunk / SIEM; retention configurable (12 months needed).
- KMS integration: customer uses AWS KMS - wants envelope encryption and key rotation guidance.
- Data residency: must pin Dedicated capacity to us-east-1 for production EU traffic currently in us-west but may request eu-west for EU customers.
- Additional ask: pen-test summary + infra network diagram for security review board.

Open questions from security review:
- Will Redwood provide SOC2 report scoped to Dedicated nodes and control descriptions for encryption at rest during runtime?
- Exact log formats and whether Redwood will redact PII from logs by default; need retention controls to be configurable per-account.
- VPC egress architecture: do we allow NAT + IP allowlist? How are outbound updates/patching handled for on-customer VPC nodes?

Commercials & pricing notes:
- Customer asked for committed block pricing vs on-demand. Suggested tiers: 4x GPU reserved pool (midsize), 12-month commit with 15% discount. Hedgerow asked for sample TCO calculation (tokens/month -> $/eng-month).
- Proposed ARR band discussed ~ $120k ARR if move to Dedicated for core product features; they want 60-day ramp/POC before full commit.

Next steps / action items:
- Deliver Dedicated VPC network diagram + SAML integration guide (owner: Ethan). (Due: 2026-03-05)
- Provide SOC2 Type II evidence and scope notes (owner: LegalOps/Security).
- Pricing: send committed tiers + sample TCO workbook (owner: Priya).
- Schedule security review call with Hedgerow SecOps (target wk of 2026-03-08).

Stitch notes (call shorthands):
- \"Hosted POC success but cost at scale\" -> leaning Dedicated for predictability.
- \"Need audit logs to Splunk and SAML Okta\" -> gating security approval.
- \"Price-sensitive; wants committed discount\" -> commercial negotiation ongoing.

Linked artifacts (stubs):
- Security questionnaire filled (drive:/Security/hedgerow/security-questionnaire-filled-2026-02-15.pdf)
- Pricing deck (drive:/Sales/hedgerow/hedgerow-dedicated-pricing-deck-v2.pdf)
- Fireflies transcripts: ff_2026-01-20_hedgerow_discussion, ff_2026-02-12_poc-demo
- Jira tickets for infra questions: RI-3421 (VPC egress), RI-3497 (SOC2 evidence request)

title: Briarcliff Response Hub
preview:
- Primary product: customer-facing support agent that deflects tickets and surfaces KB answers.
- Scale: currently 50M docs of mixed-length (kb articles, transcripts). Expected 5–10M quarterly growth.
- Queries: peak 3k QPS across distributed regions; tail latency SLO: 200ms for top-1 retrieval, 350ms for full RAG (embed+rerank) 95th.
- Interested in hybrid architecture: vector DB for coarse recall + Redwood reranker to improve top-5 precision.
- Cost sensitivity: wants predictable cost per query; target <$0.02 per query at current scale.
- Model prefs: prefer open-source embedding models (1-2B) and quantized 3-6B rerankers; open to Redwood-verified variants.
- Security: must support SSO, audit logs, regional residency (EU for EEA customers), KMS integration.
- Quote from Head of Search: "Need top-5 quality parity with our manual rerank — can we achieve 90% relevance uplift vs current BM25?"
- POC notes: POC started 2026-01-10; initial embedding pipeline built; reranker prototype improves NDCG@5 by +0.34 on internal set.
- Rep suggested dedicated pool + prefix caching to hit latency SLOs; infra team needs cost simulation.
- Next: hand off to security for SOC2 gap review; AE to push procurement schedule.

title: SignalFoundry
preview:
Summary: Mid-market product company (search + discovery in e‑commerce vertical) evaluating Redwood for relevance/rerank.

Customer goals & context:
- Replace brittle heuristic re-ranker; want ML-based rerank endpoint to rescore top-K and run offline batch scoring for weekly models.
- Primary product is an on-site search + personalized recommendations layer; rerank will be integrated in the final scoring pipeline (server-side).
- Will start on Hosted API to speed iteration, then move to Dedicated once QPS and security boxes cleared.

POC / eval criteria (explicit):
- Support for a dedicated rerank endpoint (rescore top-K lists), low per-request latency for interactive flows (target p95 <= 200ms at K=20), and cheap bulk/offline scoring for nightly model runs.
- Ability to run offline batches: need an API or job runner to score ~5M candidate pairs overnight (cost guidance requested).
- Tools for relevance tuning: can we version prompts, pin model variants, run A/B evaluations? Customer will bring own eval set (~12k labeled queries).

Notes from calls (shorthand):
- "If we can get p95 ~150-180ms at K=20 and clear pricing for 5M nightly scored rows, we'll greenlight Dedicated." — VP Product
- SE (Priya): showed demo of rerank endpoint + prefix caching; customer liked transparent latency breakdown.

Security/legal:
- Must pass basic security questionnaire (submitted 02/24). SOC2 + SSO/SAML required. Data residency not mandatory but prefer NA region only. KMS integration required for Dedicated.

Commercials & sensitivity:
- Mid-market budget; price sensitivity on offline scoring costs. Asking for committed Dedicated pricing band + burst pricing.
- Estimated ARR range provided for internal forecast: 50k-150k.

Next actions (short):
- Provide Dedicated pricing tiers that include a nightly batch scoring cost estimate (Jordan).
- Priya to scope tech POC: sample eval set import, rerank endpoint runbook, perf baseline target.
- Security team to review questionnaire answers + share SOC2 report.

Stubs/links: Fireflies ff_72619a9b (demo call), pricing draft in Drive, security answers doc.

Crib notes (fragments we use in playbook):
- "hosted-first, dedicated-later"
- "offline scoring cost is make-or-break"
- "needs versioned evals + A/B rollouts"

title: HelioFin RegTech
preview:
Account summary (regulated fintech prospect)
- HelioFin building RegTech workflows for banks (case triage + investigation summaries + policy Q&A). They are evaluating Redwood primarily for Private (EU VPC) with a path to on-prem later.
- Strong interest in Shadow Traffic quality metrics during rollouts, but only if data handling is “extremely conservative.” Multiple stakeholders repeated: “we can’t create a new copy of regulated customer data that we can’t explain to auditors.”

Primary contacts (per call notes)
- VP Platform Engineering (technical buyer) + Head of Compliance Engineering (risk owner)
- Security officer joined last 20 min of 1/22 call; asked about prompt retention, access logging, and whether Redwood staff can see samples.

Use case / workload
- Mix of chat generation and retrieval-augmented Q&A (embeddings + reranking).
- Workloads include:
  - Investigation summaries (long context, structured output)
  - Policy Q&A (format adherence important)
  - Escalation triage (safety/refusal + instruction following)
- Model rollout pain today: they don’t have safe quality gates; current approach is “A/B in staging” which doesn’t match prod traffic distributions.

Shadow traffic requirements (must-haves for HelioFin)
- Default data minimization:
  - Redact PII-like fields and customer identifiers by default (allowlist preferred over regex-only).
  - Truncate long prompts/contexts beyond a configurable max.
  - Do not store raw prompts/responses unless explicitly enabled; if stored, must be in EU region and with explicit retention window.
- Retention controls:
  - Requested defaults: 7 days retention for shadow artifacts, configurable down to 24 hours.
  - Ability to disable example storage entirely while still computing aggregates.
  - Separate retention for (a) telemetry events (aggregates), (b) sampled examples, (c) scored outputs.
- Auditability:
  - Audit log entries for: enabling shadow policy, changing sampling %, adding targets, changing retention/redaction config, exporting examples.
  - Need “who/when/what changed” for internal compliance.
- Residency:
  - EU-only storage and processing for POC; no cross-region compute for scoring.
  - If using model-graded evals, they need clarity on whether grading model runs inside same EU boundary.
- Reliability / guardrails:
  - Hard requirement: fail-open (shadow path failures cannot impact user response).
  - Timeouts/circuit breakers must be documented; asked for explicit p99 budget and how shadow QPS is capped.

Product interest / packaging signals
- They’re comfortable paying for Dedicated/Private + add-on observability/retention controls.
- They asked if Shadow Traffic is an add-on and whether scoring compute is billed separately (they want predictable cost; concerned about “double inference” spend).

Current stage details
- In evaluation with security review in progress.
- They want a scoped POC:
  - One route, two variants (control + candidate model/quant profile)
  - Sampling initially 1% with a ramp plan
  - Metrics: format adherence, refusal/safety, rubric score for instruction following, plus latency/cost deltas

Recent activity timeline
- 2025-12-18: Inbound via partner intro (EU systems integrator). Initial qualification: Private deployment required, strict residency.
- 2026-01-07: Discovery call (Platform Eng). Key note: strong interest in safe rollouts; asked if we can “shadow new model without changing user output.”
- 2026-01-22: Technical deep dive (Fireflies: 2026-01-22-technical-deep-dive-shadow-evals-and-rollouts). Discussed shadow routing + scoring pipeline; HelioFin emphasized retention/redaction/audit requirements.
- 2026-01-28: Sent security FAQ + preliminary architecture diagram; HelioFin requested a written statement of default retention for shadow artifacts.
- 2026-02-05: Follow-up Q&A (security officer). Questions on who can access sampled examples, how exports are controlled, and whether audit logs cover policy changes.
- 2026-02-20: Internal Redwood sync (Priya + Marcus + Kevin Osei) to draft “Shadow Traffic Data Handling Addendum” for regulated customers.

Risks / concerns (internal)
- HelioFin may require on-prem or air-gapped scoring for model-graded evals in later phase; we should position v1 as EU VPC-only with clear constraints.
- They will likely reject any approach that stores raw prompts by default or lacks per-tenant retention overrides.
- They are sensitive to metric cardinality: asked whether we tag by customer_id/request_id (we should explicitly state we do not emit high-cardinality identifiers in metrics).

Open questions from HelioFin
- Can they bring their own redaction rules/allowlists (configurable patterns + structured field allowlist)?
- Can they export aggregates to their SIEM/warehouse without exporting examples?
- If scoring backlog occurs, do we pause shadowing automatically? How visible is scoring lag in the dashboard?

Next steps (owner)
- Priya to send a 1–2 page written addendum covering: default retention, redaction behavior, residency, and audit log events; include guardrails summary (timeouts, circuit breaker, fail-open).
- Marcus Lin to schedule joint security + architecture review; Kevin Osei to join for audit/SIEM questions.
- If approved, scope POC in EU VPC with 1% sampling and examples storage OFF by default (aggregates only).

title: Elmwood Reserve Planning
preview:
CRM notes from discovery + workshop. Short bullets and call fragments below.

- Company: Elmwood Reserve Planning (payment/settlement platform for mid-size banks). Heavy PII, regulated EU and NA traffic.
- Primary ask: dedicated capacity with throughput guarantees and predictable unit economics for customer-facing chat assistant + background reranking pipeline.
- Security: must be able to prove SOC2 + ISO27001, KMS integration, audit logs for all inference calls, and ability to segregate EU tokens to EU region (data residency).
- Quote from CTO: 'We need predictable latency under load — financial customers won't tolerate 2s tail latencies during settlement windows.'
- Cost sensitivity: high; prefers committed reserved capacity to optimize unit cost, but wants burst capacity for marketing peaks.
- Procurement: legal wants 12-month SLA with credits and incident response matrix; finance pushing for blended ARR cap.
- Observability ask: per-route token cost, p50/p90/p99 latency by model, and per-request tracing to map back to call IDs.
- POC plan agreed: 6-week sizing pilot -> canary traffic (5%) -> full migration; will provide sample traffic logs for sizing.
- Internal stakeholders: CTO (Sarah Lowe), Head of Platform (Jamal Ortiz), Security Lead (Marta Kovac).
- Comments: SE workshop went deep on batch sizes and KV cache hit rates — they were surprised by the impact of prefix caching on effective QPS.

title: Orion Telemetry AI
preview: Summary: Edge telemetry vendor building low-latency anomaly detection + similarity search for time-series signals. Primary ask: dedicated capacity with throughput guarantees (200–400 embeddings/sec), 99th pctl latency <200ms for chat-style alerting, bulk rerank jobs overnight. POC findings: 8k context needed for some long-series aggregation prompts; KV cache cut cold-start latency by ~40ms in hosted trial. Quantization: 8-bit + mixed-4bit gave 1.9–2.4x throughput but small accuracy delta on rare classes — team worried about false positives. Preferred models: Llama-2-70B variants and a trimmed 30B for cost tiers; want ability to fall back to cheaper variant when capacity constrained. Reserved GPU ask: started with planning for 8x A100-80GB or 12x H100 depending on kernel availability. Autoscaling policy: prefer predictable reserved baseline + burst to shared pools. SLA asks: 99.9% uptime, token-level incident diagnostics, support SLO for throughput variance. Security: VPC with customer-managed KMS, audit logs, 7-year retention for event traces (compliance). Legal: need PCI-like appendix (in progress). Quotes: "We can't accept more than 15% regression in detection accuracy for quantized models". Next actions: run dedicated perf matrix (context x batch x quant) and deliver cost/latency tradeoff doc; draft SLA template; finance to sign capex 3/2026. Linked artefacts below.

title: Orthoveritas Discovery Labs
preview:
Account overview: Orthoveritas builds search/RAG for compliance and e-discovery workflows used by law firms and corporate legal. They ingest large regulatory corpora + internal policies. Primary ask: high-quality embeddings + deterministic reranking for privileged/doc-level relevance.

Sales narrative / positioning: leaning toward Redwood Private (VPC) with Optimize recommendations for quantization/batching to hit cost SLOs. Interested in Dedicated for predictable throughput during large review sprints.

Technical requirements (callouts):
- Index scale: target ~30–50M documents (mixed-size: 1k–5k tokens each) — expect ~10–25B embedding vectors after chunking.
- Latency SLO: 95th percentile <= 250ms for single-query recall+rerank flow in search UI.
- Throughput: 800–1,200 QPS during peak review periods (concurrent judge/admin workloads).
- Cost target: blended cost per query <= $0.03 (embedding+ANN+cross-encoder rerank).
- Reranking approach: two-stage — ANN recall (HNSW/IVF) -> cross-encoder reranker (smaller distil model or Redwood-verified cross-encoder), must be able to fall back to cheaper reranker during spikes.
- Accuracy/regression needs: automated evals on curated prompt sets, baseline on named-entity preservation and privilege detection.

Security & compliance:
- VPC deployment requirement (private control plane), customer-managed KMS, audit logs forwarded to SIEM, immutable retention/append-only logs for 7 years with legal-hold override.
- Need SOC2 report, ISO27001 certification, and export controls discussion (some datasets EU-residency).
- Data lifecycle: hard-delete + attestations, per-case retention policies, and ability to pause indexing for sensitive matters.

POC plan / milestones so far:
- 2026-01-14: intro call — scope agreed (dataset sample, success metrics). (ff_2026-01-14_orthoveritas_init)
- 2026-01-28: delivered ingestion template + sample embedding pipeline guidance.
- 2026-02-04: security questionnaire submitted (drive:/Orthoveritas_Security_Questionnaire_v1.pdf).
- 2026-02-18: PoC design review — agreed on two-stage rerank + candidate caching; Redwood to provide cost model (ff_2026-02-18_poc_review).
- 2026-02-24: Redwood infra team spun up private PoC environment (waiting on KMS handshake). (INF-4821)

Quotes / call excerpts:
- "We need the recall to be near perfect for privileged docs — false negatives are a legal risk." — Head of e-Discovery
- "If we can hit $0.02/query we can replace our incumbent stack." — VP Product

Open items / blockers:
- Legal needs to approve data residency clause change (EU data).
- KMS/HSM integration: scheduling with customer ops team (ETA 2 weeks).
- Quantization/regression testing: customer wants Redwood Optimize to recommend model + quantization profiles with A/B evals.

Next steps (short):
- Provide final PoC sizing doc and estimated monthly cost by 2026-03-08.
- Deliver SOC2 + ISO excerpts and KMS integration checklist to Orthoveritas security team.
- Schedule SAML test window (2-week slot) with Diego and their infra lead.

CRM shorthand / internal notes:
- Recommend canary route with small cross-encoder for daily review windows, fallback to distilled reranker for spikes.
- Watchlist: privilege detection accuracy, legal hold UX, retention SLA.

Attachments: drive:/POC_Sizing_and_Cost_Model.xlsx, drive:/MSA_Draft_2026-02-25.docx

title: Sienna Crest Commerce
preview:
Demo completed 2026-02-18. Attendees: Head of Product (Maya Lin), Eng Manager (Tom Alvarez), Security lead (Rebecca Ho), AE Jordan Blake, SE Priya Natarajan. They were enthusiastic about latency and the reranking demo (specifically preferring model variants for cost control). Key quote from Maya: 'Sharp latency and predictable cost are table stakes — we need ironclad security sign-off before pilots.' Security asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS integration diagram, and an answer about audit log retention. We provided SOC2 summary and KMS architecture (drive links above) during the demo.

Post-demo status: two follow-up emails and one Loom sent (02/19, 02/24, 03/03). No reply from Maya or Rebecca. AE left voicemail 03/05; LinkedIn message 03/09. SE offered a 2-week pilot with a dedicated SE hours (no-charge) to unblock eval; haven’t heard back. Prod team appears to have paused new pilots due to budget freeze (internal FYQ re-plan) per a short note from procurement on 02/27. No clear internal champion identified — Maya is stretched and ownership appears to have shifted to a new head of platform (not in attendee list).

Technical requirements recap: latency p95 target <150ms for chat/rerank, peak traffic ~120 QPS for search queries, embeddings throughput for nightly indexing. Model preference: open model with ability to route to cheaper quantized variants for rerank; fallbacks to smaller models during peak. Must support VPC deployment or dedicated private endpoint; require SSO/SAML, KMS for key management, and 1 year audit log retention. Cost sensitivity: high — wants clear unit economics and suggestions on batching/quantization.

Risk assessment: stalled-to-cold. High risk of closed-lost unless a new champion surfaces or procurement budget re-opens. Competitors actively engaged (we saw a follow-up from OpenAI reps on 02/26 per Maya's note).

Action items so far: delivered pricing deck and technical annex (drive links), scheduled security deep-dive offer (SE available week of 03/15), proposed 2-week pilot credit. Recommendation: preserve as warm but deprioritized — attempt one more high-value touch (security office hours + tailored pilot T&Cs) then move to nurture drip (quarterly check-ins).

title: OrchardLoop Solutions
preview:
Founder-led inbound. CEO Aisha Rahman (founder/cto) on call 02/10. Very price-sensitive — comparing Redwood hosted vs OpenAI + Anthropic. Wants a clear per-token example with embeddings + chat split.

Call highlights:
- Use-case: product Q&A + chat-based support for merchants; embed catalog + search, then chat overlay (handoff to human).
- Traffic profile: bursty (Black Friday spikes), typical ~200-300k tokens/day now, expects 2x in 6 months.
- Latency: 150-300ms per token target for chat paths; embeddings can be async.
- Cost sensitivity: founder quote: "If it costs more than OpenAI for same quality we'd struggle to justify."
- Deployment: prefers hosted API (self-serve). No immediate need for VPC.
- Security: wants retention controls (auto-delete logs after 30d), basic audit logging; asking about KMS for API keys but not mandatory now.
- Features: streaming, function-calling for structured outputs, embeddings endpoint with batch support.

Lead behavior: signed up on free credits, experimented with toy prompts. Reached out via support asking about pricing tiers and long-term committed discounts. CTO more technical; founder focused on pricing/ROI.

Sales notes / suggestions:
- Provide one-page cost calc: chat (gpt-like) @ X tokens, embeddings @ Y per 1k, show sample monthly bill for 250k tokens/day and for 1M/day.
- Highlight burst handling and autoscale, and fallback policy to smaller model during spikes.
- Call out streaming latency numbers and token-level breakdown in Console.
- Ask for product catalog size (expected docs ~120k items) to estimate embedding cost.

title: Ironbark Assurance Technologies
preview:
Account summary: Large regulated fintech focused on automated compliance tooling; evaluating an enterprise-grade IDE copilot for internal engineer productivity. Primary constraints: code never leaves VPC or on-prem boundary; strict retention and audit requirements; must integrate with customer KMS and SSO (SAML + SCIM).

Recent calls: CTO emphasized "cannot have dev code exfiltrated — full on‑prem/VPC deployment or air‑gapped option required". Security team requested example audit logs and retention policy (7 years preferred for certain records). Procurement asked for cost model for 2000 devs with steady-state 200 concurrent low-latency sessions.

Technical requirements (from SE discovery): streaming completions with <100ms perceived latency for single-line completions, 95th percentile <250ms for multi-line snippets; model-version pinning per team; canary rollouts for model updates and rollback hooks; repository-aware completions (on-prem repo access) and repo-search embeddings. Batch/update window for offline indexing once per night.

POC ask: 3-week pilot using Dedicated GPU pool (pinned model variant), VPC peering + private control plane, SSO/SAML + SCIM user sync, KMS integration for model keys, sample audit log export to Splunk. They want a failure/fallback policy: route to smaller quantized model (in-VPC) if primary capacity saturated.

Sales signals: high urgency from engineering productivity KPIs, security is gating factor. Will likely convert if security/legal sign-off and acceptable ARR.

Rep notes: keep responses concise, share network diagram template, push security FAQ and SOC2 package, loop in solutions architect for VPC design.

- latency target: p95 under 600ms for small chat sessions, throughput modest (peak 20rps).
- prefers public-cloud region us-east, but may ask for eu residency for EU customers.

title: Apex Meridian Systems
preview:
2025-10-22 — Intro call (Jordan). High-level use case: latency-sensitive RTB reranking + embeddings for targeting. Interested in Dedicated for predictable throughput.

2025-11-05 — Deep dive with SE (Priya). Discussed autoscaling, VPC peering patterns, and KMS/HSM expectations. Customer wants KMS integrated with org-wide key rotation policy.

2025-11-05 ff meeting id ff_2025-11-05_amd123 — transcript: 'we can't introduce extra billing silos, needs to show up on GCP bill' (VP infra)

2025-12-01 — Security questionnaire submitted; flagged key rotation SLA, audit log retention, and data residency for EU traffic. Security asked for SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence.

2026-01-08 — POC kicked off: Redwood Dedicated small-scale bench (4xA100 equivalent). Focus: p99 latency, throughput for 128-token requests.

2026-01-20 — POC interim: latency met targets under load but network egress policy required additional firewall rules; infra team pushed back on VPC setup timeline.

2026-01-26 — Pricing review: Redwood TCO competitive at scale but hyperscaler offered an internal discount + consolidated billing; procurement favored single-vendor billing.

2026-01-28 ff meeting id ff_2026-01-12_pq9b — Exec call: Decision leaning to GCP Vertex AI for ops simplicity. Quote: 'we have a six-figure commitment to GCP next quarter; difficult to justify another hosting contract'.

2026-01-30 — Security follow-up: KMS integration required custom work for Redwood Private; GCP managed endpoints already satisfy rotation & IAM policy out-of-the-box.

2026-02-03 — AE note: No internal champion to drive cross-team changes; infra team prioritized onboarding to existing hyperscaler tools vs. new vendor onboarding.

2026-02-09 — Procurement email thread (thread-1623345): final selection = GCP Vertex AI endpoints. Redwood marked closed-lost. Reason summary: hyperscaler integration + existing discounts + lower apparent ops overhead.

Action items: Capture technical gaps (KMS onboarding docs, VPC quickstart), schedule post-mortem, add to competitive win/loss board.

Open risks for future: if they decouple from GCP billing or require private deployment for regulated lines, re-engage; track their KMS rotation policy changes.

Notes style: fragments common, some paraphrased customer quotes, repeated points: billing consolidation, internal infra preference, lack of champion.
